notes: User messages are extremely terse (avg ~5 words) — 'My own', 'Okay do that', 'Do all 8'. All the technical substance lives in assistant messages and tool calls. The architectural concept being worked through is: MCP servers probed by Smithery for resources/list/prompts/list must register empty handlers to avoid -32601 Method-not-found errors, even when they don't expose resources or prompts. Rolled across four repos (goldenpipe/goldencheck/goldenflow/goldenmatch). Forbidden slugs guard against distractors that the model might latch onto (a leaked Smithery API key that the user pasted, and repeated `gh auth switch` commands between personal/work GitHub accounts) — these are operational noise, not architectural concepts.
